mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in real retail sales (RSAFS) over the prior three months signals robust consumer demand, which translates into higher expected corporate revenues and positive S&P 500 forward 20-day returns.
failure_condition: The 20-day forward return following a positive 3-month RSAFS change is not significantly greater than zero (t-test p > 0.05) over a 3-year rolling window, or the Rank IC between RSAFS momentum and 20-day returns falls below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid rise in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.3 percentage points over a three-month window signals deteriorating labor market conditions that precede a demand-driven earnings contraction, producing negative S&P 500 forward 5-day returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 5-day returns in more than 50% of instances where the UNRATE trigger is activated, or the walk-forward Rank IC is not negative and significant (p > 0.05) over any 2-year period.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is elevated relative to the inflation level (FEDFUNDS > 3.5% while CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized change is below 2%), restrictive real monetary policy jointly depresses equity valuations through a higher discount rate and falling terminal growth expectations, producing negative S&P 500 forward 60-day returns.
failure_condition: Triggered instances yield positive 60-day forward returns in more than 45% of cases, or the joint interaction term fails to outperform the standalone FEDFUNDS signal in a nested regression (F-test p > 0.05) over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: During late-cycle expansions, month-over-month industrial production (INDPRO) declines signal that supply capacity constraints are binding and margin compression is imminent, leading to negative S&P 500 forward 20-day returns.
failure_condition: In identified late-cycle quarters, INDPRO contractions are followed by positive 20-day returns in more than 50% of instances, or the conditional Sharpe ratio of the signal is not statistically lower than the unconditional Sharpe (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A sharp drop in housing starts (HOUST) below 1,200 thousand units signals a collapse in residential investment that drags construction-related earnings and broader demand expectations, producing negative S&P 500 forward 60-day returns.
failure_condition: When HOUST is below 1200, S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of triggered months, or the conditional mean return is not significantly below zero (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate above 4.5% chokes corporate borrowing and buyback activity, reducing equity demand and producing negative S&P 500 forward 5-day returns.
failure_condition: FEDFUNDS > 4.5% episodes are followed by positive 5-day returns in more than 50% of cases, or the cumulative return during the tightening cycle is not significantly negative (bootstrap p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A positive three-month trend in non-farm payrolls (PAYEMS) signals expanding labor income that supports consumption and corporate top-line growth, generating positive S&P 500 forward 20-day returns.
failure_condition: PAYEMS 3-month momentum fails to show a positive Rank IC with 20-day forward returns (IC < 0.03) over any 3-year rolling window, or the mean 20-day return following positive momentum is not significantly above zero (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When quarterly real GDP (GDPC1) growth accelerates while industrial production (INDPRO) simultaneously contracts, the divergence signals an economy tilting toward services-driven overheating that is vulnerable to a monetary policy overhang, producing negative S&P 500 forward 60-day returns.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter growth > 0.5% and INDPRO month-over-month change < 0
failure_condition: The joint condition of rising GDPC1 and falling INDPRO is followed by positive 60-day returns in more than 50% of instances, or the interaction term adds no explanatory power beyond the main effects in a panel regression (F-test p > 0.05).
